# azurefoundry_agent (Resource)

Manages an Azure AI Foundry Agent.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "azurefoundry_agent" "support_assistant" {
  name         = "support-assistant"
  description  = "Tier-1 customer support agent"
  model        = "gpt-4o"
  instructions = "You are a helpful customer support assistant. Cite ticket numbers when summarizing."
  temperature  = 0.7

  tools {
    type = "code_interpreter"
  }

  tools {
    type = "file_search"
  }

  metadata = {
    environment = "production"
    owner       = "support-team"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `model` (String) The model deployment name (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4o-mini`).

### Optional

- `code_interpreter_file_ids` (List of String) File IDs available to the code_interpreter tool.
- `description` (String) A short description of the agent.
- `file_search_vector_store_ids` (List of String) Vector store IDs (from `azurefoundry_vector_store`) to attach to the `file_search` tool. Maximum 1.
- `instructions` (String) The system prompt for the agent.
- `metadata` (Map of String) Up to 16 key/value string pairs.
- `name` (String) Display name for the agent.
- `temperature` (Number) Sampling temperature between 0 and 2.
- `tools` (Block List) Tools enabled for the agent. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--tools))
- `top_p` (Number) Nucleus sampling parameter between 0 and 1.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (Number) Unix timestamp when the agent was created.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the agent.

<a id="nestedblock--tools"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tools`

Required:

- `type` (String) Tool type: `code_interpreter`, `file_search`, `bing_grounding`, `azure_ai_search`, `azure_function`.
